decisions.description: Extend JWT permission claims from flat codename strings to a dict mapping each permission codename to either specific channel slugs or ['*'] for global access.
Treat existing flat-string permission claims as equivalent to all-channel access for backward compatibility with already-issued tokens.
Extend the check_permissions interception point on mutation base classes to validate the user's scoped permissions against the target resource's channel.
For mutations on resources with a channel foreign key (e.g. orderUpdate), resolve the channel from the instance and check it against the JWT's scoped permissions.
Gate the checkoutComplete mutation's permission check before any side effects (order creation, payment processing, webhooks) so a late failure cannot leave a half-created order.
Add a channel_access relationship on the App model so third-party apps can only access channels they are explicitly installed for.
Scope the dashboard staff-user products query so it no longer returns products from channels the user is not permitted to access.
Write migration scripts that default all existing permission groups to all-channel access when the new scoped-permissions model ships.

action_items.text: Draft the RFC for the JWT structure and the modified check_permissions flow; deliver a feature branch with migration scripts by end of sprint
Perform the security review on the new permission model
Coordinate the dashboard UI changes for channel-scoped permission assignment
